extracted_codes.display: Other specified cerebral degenerations in childhood

extracted_codes.context: Control 2: Patients without any history of any Psychiatry ICD-9 codes (290-319), and not seen in any Neurology, Developmental Psychology or Psychology departments

clinical_criteria: DSM-IV Autistic Disorder: At least 2 unique from Social Interaction criteria, 1 from Communication criteria, 1 from Behavior, Interests and Activities criteria = total at least 6 elements
DSM-IV Asperger's Syndrome: At least 2 unique from Social Interaction criteria, 1 from Behavior, Interests and Activities criteria + no language delay + average cognitive function
DSM-IV PDD-NOS: More than one element from one or more categories of Social Interaction/Communication/Behavior, Interests and Activities
DSM-V criteria A: All 3 of A1 (deficits in social-emotional reciprocity), A2 (deficits in nonverbal communicative behaviors), A3 (deficits in developing/maintaining relationships)
DSM-V criteria B: At least 2 from B1 (stereotyped/repetitive behaviors), B2 (excessive adherence to routines), B3 (highly restricted interests), B4 (hyper/hypo-reactivity to sensory input)
DSM-V criteria C: Symptoms must be present in early childhood
DSM-V criteria D: Symptoms together limit and impair everyday functioning
algorithm_summary: This autism phenotype algorithm uses a multi-step approach: 1) Include patients with autism spectrum disorder ICD-9 codes (299.0, 299.80, 299.9), 2) Exclude patients with specific conditions (299.1, 295*, 330.8, 759.5, 759.83), 3) Apply DSM-IV symptom criteria for three subtypes (Autistic Disorder, Asperger's, PDD-NOS) based on counts of symptoms across Social Interaction, Communication, and Behavior/Interests/Activities domains, 4) Apply DSM-V criteria requiring all social communication deficits plus at least 2 restricted/repetitive behaviors with early onset and functional impairment. Controls include patients without ASD codes or broader psychiatric codes/specialty visits.
